document.diagnosis: The established five-bank three-review mechanism remains champion: Iteration 30 raises its pooled exact record from 140/336 to 146/354, while the supplied pre-checkpoint weakest-family rate was 37.5%. The checkpoint again identifies logic as the immediate weakness: every reviewed strategy scored only 1/6 logic. Selection is the larger general bottleneck when candidates exist: five-bank repair, cross-examination, and one-review repair each left three oracle-covered cases unconverted, while the nine-bank selector converted only 5/11 covered cases. Cross-examination won the panel at 7/18 and had the best partial accuracy (75.8%), but its seven helpful interventions included one harmful reversal. Parallel three-review repair was safer at five helpful and zero harmful; one-review repair preserved a positive 4–0 balance for two fewer calls. The next batch should therefore replicate the champion and current winner, retain the efficient repair comparison, and continue the uncompressed nine-bank selection probe without adding selector depth.
extensions_requested: Add a shared-evidence-board primitive: three independent reviewers post candidate-indexed decisive claims and falsification attempts to a sealed, frequency-hidden board, then one terminal integrator chooses or repairs an answer with base-plurality fallback. This domain-neutral four-call architecture would test whether explicit evidence aggregation closes oracle-to-final gaps better than either parallel voting or sequential proposal passing.
most_important_evidence: All four reviewed systems had only 1/6 logic accuracy in Iteration 30; the five-answer base bank contained no correct logic candidate, and repair expanded logic coverage to only 1/6 or 2/6.
Cross-examination led Iteration 30 at 7/18 with seven helpful and one harmful intervention, versus 6/18 and a 5–0 intervention balance for the pooled champion.
The nine-bank selector had 11/18 oracle coverage but only 5/18 final accuracy, leaving a six-case conversion gap and making two harmful reversals.
Five-bank three-review repair, cross-examination, and one-review repair respectively had expanded-oracle/final results of 9/6, 10/7, and 8/5: each left three covered cases unresolved.
The one-review repair used six calls per case versus eight for the champion while retaining four helpful and zero harmful interventions on the checkpoint.
The complete proposed batch costs at most 204 calls: 12×9 for the shared largest base bank plus 12×(3+3+1+1) review calls, below the 300-call ceiling.

proposals.hypothesis: Retain the minimum-cost single-answer baseline unchanged so pooled gains remain interpretable.
proposals.name: Direct One

proposals.hypothesis: Retain deterministic five-answer plurality unchanged as the shared-bank aggregation control.
proposals.name: Five-Vote Plurality

proposals.hypothesis: Retain the established pooled champion unchanged; its long-run weakest-family record and safer positive correction balance remain the primary benchmark.
proposals.name: Efficient Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

proposals.hypothesis: Retain the current panel winner unchanged to test whether sequential falsification continues to outperform independent parallel repair rather than reflecting fresh-panel variance.
proposals.name: Sequential Five-Bank Cross-Examination

proposals.hypothesis: Continue the matched efficiency probe: one answer-generating falsifier costs two fewer calls per case and retained a 4–0 helpful-to-harmful balance on the checkpoint.
proposals.name: One-Review Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

proposals.hypothesis: Continue the cheaper full-bank candidate-only selector to measure whether a single falsifier can reliably convert the nine-bank coverage advantage; its large current oracle gap makes this a direct selector stress test.
proposals.name: Nine-Bank One-Selector Falsifying Selection

research_hypotheses: Sequential cross-examination may improve partial and exact integration over parallel repair, but another panel is needed to determine whether its harmful-reversal risk is systematic.
A single answer-generating reviewer may preserve most beneficial corrections at materially lower call cost than a three-review plurality.
Nine independent candidates improve oracle coverage, but one candidate-only selector may be too variable to exploit that coverage consistently.
Explicitly aggregating falsifiable evidence, rather than merely adding candidates or reviewer votes, is the most motivated next orthogonal primitive.
document.risk_note: The batch is deliberately confirmatory because four of six slots are mechanically retained. Cross-examination and nine-bank selection both caused harmful reversals in Iteration 30, and logic candidate scarcity means no current selector can recover answers that are never generated. Treat any single-panel winner as development evidence, not a new champion.
